description: Polygon — slim workflow by default, multi-LLM activity log, package renamed to @subaru486/polygon.
changelog:
**Polygon 0.6.0.** The fork gets its own name: the npm package is now `@subaru486/polygon` (CLI commands `polygon` / `trellis` / `tl` all work).

**Slim workflow is the default.**
- `trellis init -y` deploys the lightweight claude+codex setup: workflow.md is a single ~290-line platform-neutral guide (multi-platform walkthrough variants removed from the deployed copy; other platform flags still work and receive the generic content).
- Inline-first: the main agent edits code directly by default. Forced sub-agent dispatch, override phrase allowlists, anti-rationalization tables and jsonl gates are removed or downgraded to advisory.
- Context injection slimmed: SessionStart payload 27 KB → ~2 KB; per-turn breadcrumbs are 2-4 lines per status. Oversized skills cut to the bone (brainstorm 538 → 57 lines).

**Multi-LLM activity log.**
- `activity.jsonl` per task records who/which platform/model did what, when — `task.py start` and `task.py archive` stamp automatically; `task.py activity-append` / `activity-log` for manual entries and the timeline.
- `task.json` rolls agents up into `meta.agents[]`; journal sessions record an **Agent**: platform/model line.
- workflow.md gains an Activity Log section with a cross-LLM handoff protocol.
notes: Breaking only in defaults, not in files: existing instances keep working; `trellis update` refreshes scripts/skills in place and adds activity-log support. No file migrations. Users of the old `@subaru486/trellis` package should `npm i -g @subaru486/polygon` (the old package will not receive further updates).
